task: feat: validate entry timing guard activation
question: Does applying adjustedAction (HIGH risk BUY/STRONG_BUY -> WAIT_PULLBACK, WATCH/HOLD -> CAUTION) instead of the original action actually improve outcomes, and is it safe to activate in the live recommendation engine?

method.step1_naiveReplay: Compare metrics on all evaluated journal trades (baseline) vs. the same set with HIGH-risk-flagged trades removed (activeGuard), reusing the existing _before_after() replay in entry_timing_safety.py.
method.step2_lookaheadCheck: Added in this task: for every HIGH-risk (affected) trade, check whether its entryTimingRiskScore reasons include ANY criterion that is only knowable AFTER the trade plays out (MAE_DEEPER_THAN_MFE, STOP_TOO_TIGHT, STOP_BEFORE_TARGET, OVEREXTENDED_ENTRY, MARKET_GAP), versus criteria knowable AT RECOMMENDATION TIME (OVEREXTENSION_RISK_HIGH, LOW_MOMENTUM_WITH_OVEREXTENSION, derived from overextensionRisk/momentumContinuationScore/setupScore).
method.step3_dataCoverageCheck: Checked how many evaluated journal rows actually carry overextensionRisk/momentumContinuationScore/setupScore values at all (these are display-only diagnostic fields produced by data_loader.py at recommendation time, separate from raw_recommendation's stored fields).

step1_naiveReplayResults.interpretation: On its face this looks like a clean win in every dimension and every market. This is exactly why it required the look-ahead check below before any activation decision.

reasonReasonFrequencyAmongHighRiskTrades.MAE_DEEPER_THAN_MFE: 410/410 (100%) - requires the realized adverse excursion during the actual holding period

step2_lookaheadBiasCheck.conclusion: By construction, OVEREXTENSION_RISK_HIGH (+2) and LOW_MOMENTUM_WITH_OVEREXTENSION (+1) are the only two scoring criteria that do not require knowing the trade's future price path. Their maximum combined contribution is 3 points, which is below the HIGH threshold of 4. Therefore no trade can ever reach HIGH risk using only data available at the moment a live recommendation is issued -- every HIGH classification necessarily includes at least one criterion derived from the realized outcome (MAE/MFE/failureReason).

step3_dataCoverageCheck.conclusion: Independent of the scoring-threshold issue above, the prospective fields are not even populated in the merged virtual_trade_journal evaluation rows in the current data pipeline (raw_recommendation snapshots captured at journal-write time do not carry overextensionRisk/momentumContinuationScore/setupScore keys). In current operation, entryTimingRiskScore is computed 100% from outcome-only data (MAE, MFE, failureReason), 0% from recommendation-time features.

decision.rationale: This is a stronger finding than 'ambiguous results, keep cautious': the apparent improvement in step1 is not a forecast of what an active guard would do, because the guard cannot be computed before the outcome exists. Activating it as designed is not merely risky -- it is not implementable as a live, prospective gate. The step1 numbers reflect retrospectively excluding trades whose bad outcome is already known, not a predictive filter.

recommendedNextStep: To build a genuinely activatable entry-timing guard: (1) persist overextensionRisk/momentumContinuationScore/setupScore into virtual_trade_journal evaluation rows at journal-write time (currently dropped before merge), (2) design a new risk score using ONLY recommendation-time features (the existing OVEREXTENSION_RISK_HIGH/LOW_MOMENTUM_WITH_OVEREXTENSION criteria plus possibly entry_distance/RSI from dsg_signal_engine.py's chase-risk classifier), (3) validate that prospective-only score against historical evaluated outcomes as a true held-out predictor (bucket by score, check stop-failure rate / return monotonicity) before ever gating a live action.
codeChanges: mone-web-app/backend/app/services/entry_timing_safety.py: added _lookahead_bias_check() and wired it into _activation_decision() and build_entry_timing_diagnostics(); activationReason now states the look-ahead bias finding instead of a generic 'criteria passed but unimplemented' message; new lookaheadBiasCheck field exposed in /api/virtual/entry-timing-diagnostics and /api/journal/entry-timing-diagnostics responses (and empty_response()).
overfitControls: look-ahead bias explicitly checked per-trade, not assumed
no change to _final_score, ranking, filters, EV, candidates, or entry/target/stop formulas
no change to live recommendation action decision (final_engine.py untouched)
